medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Patient recently admitted with hypertensive emergency. Switched the hours of his medications. Took some (amlodipine and others) at 10 PM and had a SBP of 230/120. His BP improved after taking labetalol 600 mg PO at midnight but experienced oppressive left lower chest pain. Denied diaphoresis, nausea, or vomiting. In the ED, BP was 162/88 mmHg, HR 74, RR 16. Received morphine (2mg IV once) with good effect. Initial diagnostic findings showed no changes on ECG and negative first set of cardiac enzymes.
medical_history.past_medical_history: Hypertension, CAD, diabetes, dyslipidemia, obstructive sleep apnea, anxiety, history of prostate cancer, neurocysticercosis, and more. No major surgical procedures.
medical_history.family_history: Father with CAD, died of MI at age Not specified. Mother died of sepsis. 5 siblings died young; etiology unknown. No family history of arrhythmias.

physical_examination.general_examination: Vital signs: Temp 97.2 F, BP 190/96 mmHg, HR 70 BPM, RR 18, O2 saturation 98% on 2 L NC. Well-appearing man in NAD, oriented x3.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Neurological examination: awake, A&Ox3, CNs II-XII grossly intact. Cardiac examination: RRR, normal S1, decreased S2, SEM in RUSB. Lungs CTA bilat, respiratory effort unlabored.
